briefdescription.para: Address Register Offset.
Size Register Offset.
Status Register Offset.
Control Register Offset.
Interrupt Status Register Offset.

sectiondef.header: Size register bit masks and shifts

briefdescription.para: Mask for size.
Last word check bit mask.
Shift for size.
FIFO overflow mask, it is valid only to Destination Channel.
Invalid APB access mask.
FIFO threshold hit indicator mask.
Time out counter expired to access memory mask.
Time out counter expired to access stream mask.
AXI Read/Write error mask.
Done mask.
Memory done mask, it is valid only for source channel.

((XCSUDMA_IXR_INVALID_APB_MASK)| (XCSUDMA_IXR_FIFO_THRESHHIT_MASK) | (XCSUDMA_IXR_TIMEOUT_MEM_MASK) | (XCSUDMA_IXR_TIMEOUT_STRM_MASK) | (XCSUDMA_IXR_AXI_WRERR_MASK) | (XCSUDMA_IXR_DONE_MASK) | (XCSUDMA_IXR_MEM_DONE_MASK))

detaileddescription.para: All interrupt mask for source

briefdescription.para: ((XCSUDMA_IXR_FIFO_OVERFLOW_MASK) | (XCSUDMA_IXR_INVALID_APB_MASK) | (XCSUDMA_IXR_FIFO_THRESHHIT_MASK) | (XCSUDMA_IXR_TIMEOUT_MEM_MASK) | (XCSUDMA_IXR_TIMEOUT_STRM_MASK) | (XCSUDMA_IXR_AXI_WRERR_MASK) | (XCSUDMA_IXR_DONE_MASK))

detaileddescription.para: All interrupt mask for destination

briefdescription.para: SW timeout loop value for transfer completion.
Source Channel of CSU_DMA.
Destination Channel of CSU_DMA.
This typedef contains CSU_DMA Channel Types.
This function initializes CSU_DMA core.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior using a CSU_DMA core. Initialization of an CSU_DMA includes setting up the instance data and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XCsuDma instance.
Reference to a structure containing information about a specific XCsuDma instance.

Device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, pass in the physical address instead.

memberdef.argsstring: (XCsuDma *InstancePtr, XCsuDma_Channel Channel, u64 Addr, u32 Size, u8 EnDataLast)
memberdef.name: XCsuDma_Transfer

briefdescription.para: This function sets the starting address and size of the data to be transferred from/to the memory through the AXI interface.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL
64 bit variable which holds the starting address of data which needs to write into the memory(DST) (or read from the memory(SRC)).
32 bit variable which represents the number of 4 byte words needs to be transferred from starting address.

Triggers an end of the message. Enables or disables data_inp_last signal to stream interface when current command is completed. It is applicable only to source channel; ignored for destination channel.
1 - Asserts data_inp_last signal.
0 - data_inp_last will not be asserted.

simplesect.para: Data_inp_last signal is asserted simultaneously with the data_inp_valid signal associated with the final 32-bit word transfer.

memberdef.argsstring: (XCsuDma *InstancePtr, XCsuDma_Channel Channel, u32 AddrLow, u32 AddrHigh, u32 Size, u8 EnDataLast)
memberdef.name: XCsuDma_64BitTransfer

briefdescription.para: This function sets the starting address and size of the data to be transferred from/to the memory through the AXI interface.

detaileddescription.para: This function is useful for pmu processor to execute a 64-bit DMA transfer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL
Bit variable which holds the starting lower address of data which needs to write into the memory(DST) (or read from the memory(SRC)).
32 bit variable which holds the higher address of data which needs to write into the memory(DST) (or read from the memoroy(SRC)).
32 bit variable which represents the number of 4 byte words to be transferred from starting address.

Trigger an end of message. Enables or disable data_inp_last signal to stream interface when current command is completed. Only applicable for the source channel; ignored for destination channel.
1 - Asserts data_inp_last signal.
0 - data_inp_last will not be asserted.

simplesect.para: Data_inp_last signal is asserted simultaneously with the data_inp_valid signal associated with the final 32-bit word transfer. This API does not invalidate the DMA buffer. It is recommended to call this API only through PMU processor.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current address location of the memory, from where it has to read the data(SRC) or the location where it has to write the data (DST) based on the channel selection.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

simplesect.para:
Address is a 64 bit variable which holds the current address.
From this location data has to be read(SRC)
At this location data has to be written(DST)

briefdescription.para: This function returns the size of the data yet to be transferred from memory to CSU_DMA or CSU_DMA to memory based on the channel selection.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

simplesect.para: Returns number of bytes of data yet to be transferred.

briefdescription.para: This function pauses the Channel data transfer to/from memory or to/from stream based on pause type.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

Type of pause to be enabled.
XCSUDMA_PAUSE_MEMORY(0) - Pause memory
SRC stops issuing new read commands to memory.
DST stops issuing new write commands to memory.
XCSUDMA_PAUSE_STREAM(1) - Pause stream
SRC stops the transfer of data from FIFO to Stream.
DST stops the transfer of data from stream to FIFO.

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether Channel memory/stream is paused based on the given pause type.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

Type of the pause which needs to be checked.
XCSUDMA_PAUSE_MEMORY(0) - Pause memory
SRC stops issuing new read commands to memory.
DST stops issuing new write commands to memory.
XCSUDMA_PAUSE_STREAM(1) - Pause stream
SRC stops the transfer of data from FIFO to Stream.
DST stops the transfer of data from stream to FIFO.

simplesect.para:
Returns the pause status.
TRUE if it is in paused state.
FALSE if it is not in pause state.

briefdescription.para: This function resumes the channel if it is in paused state and continues where it has left.

detaileddescription.para: Based on the type of pause, there is no effect if it is not in paused state.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

Type of pause to be resumed if it is paused.
XCSUDMA_PAUSE_MEMORY(0) - Pause memory
SRC stops issuing new read commands to memory.
DST stops issuing new write commands to memory.
XCSUDMA_PAUSE_STREAM(1) - Pause stream
SRC stops the transfer of data from FIFO to Stream.
DST stops the transfer of data from stream to FIFO.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the sum of all the data read from AXI memory.

detaileddescription.para: It is valid only when using the CSU_DMA source channel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The sum of all the data read from memory.
Before the transfer starts, clear this register to get the correct sum. Otherwise, it adds to previous value which results in an incorrect output. Valid only for source channel.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the check sum of the data read from AXI memory.

detaileddescription.para: It is valid only for CSU_DMA source channel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The sum of all the data read from memory.
Before the transfer starts, clear this register to get correct sum. Otherwise, it adds to previous value which results in an incorrect output.

briefdescription.para: This function polls for completion of data transfer periodically until the DMA done bit set or until the timeout occurs.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS - In case of Success XST_FAILURE - In case of Timeout.

briefdescription.para: This function configures all the values of CSU_DMA Channels with the values of updated XCsuDma_Configure structure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

Pointer to the structure XCsuDma_Configure whose values are used to configure CSU_DMA core.
SssFifoThesh: When the DST FIFO level >= this value, the SSS interface signal, "data_out_fifo_level_hit" is asserted. This mechanism can be used by the SSS to flow control data that is being looped back from the SRC DMA.
Range is (0x10 to 0x7A) threshold is 17 to 123 entries.
It is valid only for DST CSU_DMA IP.
ApbErr: When accessed to invalid APB the resulting pslerr will be
0 - 1'b0
1 - 1'b1
EndianType: Type of endianness
0 doesn't change order
1 will flip the order.
AxiBurstType: Type of the burst
0 will issue INCR type burst
1 will issue FIXED type burst
TimeoutValue: Time out value for timers
0x000 to 0xFFE are valid inputs
0xFFF clears both timers
FifoThresh: Programmed watermark value
Range is 0x00 to 0x80 (0 to 128 entries).
Acache: Sets the AXI CACHE bits on the AXI Write/Read channel.
Cacheable ARCACHE[1] for SRC Channel and AWCACHE[1] for DST channel are always 1, we need to configure remaining 3 signal support (Bufferable, Read allocate and Write allocate). Valid inputs are:
0x000 - Cacheable, but do not allocate
0x001 - Cacheable and bufferable, but do not allocate
0x010 - Cacheable write-through, allocate on reads only
0x011 - Cacheable write-back, allocate on reads only
0x100 - Cacheable write-through, allocate on writes only
0x101 - Cacheable write-back, allocate on writes only
0x110 - Cacheable write-through, allocate on both reads and writes
0x111 - Cacheable write-back, allocate on both reads and writes
RouteBit: To select route
0 : Command will be routed normally
1 : Command will be routed to APU's cache controller
TimeoutEn: To enable or disable time out counters
0 : The 2 Timeout counters are disabled
1 : The 2 Timeout counters are enabled
TimeoutPre: Set the prescaler value for the timeout in clk (~2.5ns) cycles
Range is 0x000(Prescaler enables timer every cycles) to 0xFFF(Prescaler enables timer every 4096 cycles)
MaxOutCmds: Controls the maximumum number of outstanding AXI read commands issued.
Range is 0x0(Up to 1 Outstanding Read command allowed) to 0x8 (Up to 9 Outstanding Read command allowed)

simplesect.para: To use timers timeout value Timeout enable field should be enabled. Users should check for the status of existing transfers before making configuration changes.

briefdescription.para: This function updates XCsuDma_Configure structure members with the configured values of CSU_DMA's Channel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

Pointer to the structure XCsuDma_Configure whose members are updated with configurations of CSU_DMA core.
SssFifoThesh: When the DST FIFO level >= this value, the SSS interface signal, "data_out_fifo_level_hit" will be asserted. This mechanism can be used by the SSS to flow control data that is being looped back from the SRC DMA.
Range is (0x10 to 0x7A) threshold is 17 to 123 entries.
It is valid only for DST CSU_DMA IP.
ApbErr: When accessed to invalid APB the resulting pslerr will be
0 - 1'b0
1 - 1'b1
EndianType: Type of endianness
0 doesn't change order
1 will flip the order.
AxiBurstType: Type of the burst
0 will issue INCR type burst
1 will issue FIXED type burst
TimeoutValue: Time out value for timers
0x000 to 0xFFE are valid inputs
0xFFF clears both timers
FifoThresh: Programmed watermark value
Range is 0x00 to 0x80 (0 to 128 entries).
Acache: Sets the AXI CACHE bits on the AXI Write/Read channel.
Cacheable ARCACHE[1] for SRC Channel and AWCACHE[1] for DST channel are always 1, we need to configure remaining 3 signal support (Bufferable, Read allocate and Write allocate). Valid inputs are:
0x000 - Cacheable, but do not allocate
0x001 - Cacheable and bufferable, but do not allocate
0x010 - Cacheable write-through, allocate on reads only
0x011 - Cacheable write-back, allocate on reads only
0x100 - Cacheable write-through, allocate on writes only
0x101 - Cacheable write-back, allocate on writes only
0x110 - Cacheable write-through, allocate on both reads and writes
0x111 - Cacheable write-back, allocate on both reads and writes
RouteBit: To select route
0 : Command will be routed based normally
1 : Command will be routed to APU's cache controller
TimeoutEn: To enable or disable time out counters
0 : The 2 Timeout counters are disabled
1 : The 2 Timeout counters are enabled
TimeoutPre: Set the prescaler value for the timeout in clk (~2.5ns) cycles
Range is 0x000(Prescaler enables timer every cycles) to 0xFFF(Prescaler enables timer every 4096 cycles)
MaxOutCmds: Controls the maximumum number of outstanding AXI read commands issued.
Range is 0x0(Up to 1 Outstanding Read command allowed) to 0x8 (Up to 9 Outstanding Read command allowed)

briefdescription.para: This function returns interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCSUDMA_IXR_*_MASK constants defined in xcsudma_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

simplesect.para: The pending interrupts of the CSU_DMA. Use th following masks to interpret the returned value. XCSUDMA_IXR_SRC_MASK - For Source channel XCSUDMA_IXR_DST_MASK - For Destination channel

briefdescription.para: This function clears interrupt(s).

detaileddescription.para: Every bit set in Interrupt Status Register indicates that a specific type of interrupt is occurring, and this function clears one or more interrupts by writing a bit mask to Interrupt Clear Register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL
Mask to clear. Bit positions of 1 will be cleared. Bit positions of 0 will not change the previous interrupt status. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCSUDMA_IXR_* bits defined in xcsudma_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the interrupt(s).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCSUDMA_IXR_*_MASK constants defined in xcsudma_hw.h to create the bit-mask to enable interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

Contains interrupts to be enabled.
Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCSUDMA_IXR_*_MASK bits defined in xcsudma_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the interrupt(s).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCSUDMA_IXR_*_MASK constants defined in xcsudma_hw.h to create the bit-mask to disable interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

Contains interrupts to be disabled.
Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCSUDMA_IXR_*_MASK bits defined in xcsudma_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the interrupt mask to know which interrupts are enabled and which of them were disabled.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XCsuDma instance to be worked on.
Type of channel Source channel - XCSUDMA_SRC_CHANNEL Destination Channel - XCSUDMA_DST_CHANNEL

simplesect.para:
The current interrupt mask. The mask indicates which interrupts are enabled/disabled.
0 bit represents that the corresponding interrupt is enabled.
1 bit represents that the Corresponding interrupt is disabled. To interpret returned mask use
XCSUDMA_IXR_SRC_MASK that the For source channel
XCSUDMA_IXR_DST_MASK that the For destination channel

briefdescription.para: This function runs a self-test on the driver and hardware device.

detaileddescription.para: Performs reset of both source and destination channels and checks if reset is working properly or not.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XCsuDma instance.

listitem.para:
XST_SUCCESS if the self-test passed.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID/BaseAddress, or NULL if no match is found.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Transfer size conversion to bytes for Versal Net.
Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Input operation.
Output operation.
This macro reads the given register.

parameterdescription.para: Base address of the CSU_DMA core.
Register offset of the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XCsuDma_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: This macro writes the value into the given register.

parameterdescription.para: Base address of the CSU_DMA core.
Register offset of the register.
32-bit value to write to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XCsuDma_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: This section contains the functions of the CSU_DMA driver.

detaileddescription.para: The xcsudma_sinit.c file contains static initialization methods for Xilinx CSU_DMA core.
The xcsudma_selftest.c file contains a diagnostic self-test function for the CSU_DMA driver.
The xcsudma_intr.c file contains interrupt related functions of Xilinx CSU_DMA core.
The xcsudma_hw.h header file contains identifiers and register-level driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the CSU_DMA core.
Refer to the header file xcsudma.h for more detailed information.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vnsld 22/10/14 First release 1.1 adk 10/05/16 Fixed CR#951040 race condition in the recv path when source and destination points to the same buffer. 1.4 Nava 1/10/19 Added PSU_PMU processor check to skip the Flushing cache memory and Invalidating cache memory API's for PMU Microblaze platform. Rama 02/26/19 Fixed IAR issue by changing "XCsuDma_WaitForDoneTimeout" to function arc 03/26/19 Fixed MISRA-C violations. 1.5 aru 07/05/19 Fixed coverity warning. 1.6 aru 08/29/19 Added assert check in XCsuDma_WaitForDoneTimeout(). 1.6 rm 11/05/19 Modified usleep waitloop and timeout value in XCsuDma_WaitForDoneTimeout(). 1.7 hk 08/03/20 Reorganize transfer function to accommodate all processors and cache functionality. 1.7 sk 08/26/20 Fix MISRA-C violations. 1.7 sk 08/26/20 Remove busy check in SetConfig. 1.11 bm 02/21/22 Add byte-aligned support for VERSAL_NET devices 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Replace driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Update Overview section based on review comments. 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Update XCsuDma_GetSize return type description. 1.14 ab 01/16/23 Added Xil_WaitForEvent() to XcsuDma_WaitForDoneTimeout. 1.14 ab 01/18/23 Added byte-aligned transfer API for VERSAL_NET devices. 1.14 bm 05/01/23 Fixed Assert condition in XCsuDma_Transfer for VERSAL_NET. 2.0 sd 11/10/25 Added support for VERSAL_2VP_P devices.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vnsld 22/10/14 First release 1.9 sk 02/11/21 Add description for XPS_CRP_BASEADDRESS and XCSUDMA_DONE_TIMEOUT_VAL macros. 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Replace driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Update Overview section based on review comments. 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Add cond INTERNAL to avoid internal macros. 1.12 sk 03/03/22 Added support for VERSAL NET. 1.16 ng 08/20/24 Added spartanup device support 2.0 sd 11/10/25 Added support for VERSAL_2VP_P devices.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the header file xcsudma.h for more details of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vnsld 22/10/14 First release 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Replace driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Update Overview section based on review comments.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the header file xcsudma.h for detailed information.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vnsld 22/10/14 First release 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Replace driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Update Overview section based on review comments.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vnsld 22/10/14 First release 1.7 sk 08/26/20 Fix MISRA-C violations. 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Replace driver version in addtogroup with Overview. 1.11 sk 03/03/22 Update Overview section based on review comments.
